 succeeded in 761ms:
   1: """I-arch-001d — bridge from pipeline-A canonical artifact_dir to slice-chain
   2: Pydantic models (ScopeDecision, EvidencePool, VerifiedReport) consumable by
   3: the audit-bundle POST endpoint.
   4: 
   5: The audit-bundle path expects the slice-001/002/003 Pydantic shapes, but
   6: pipeline-A produces a different artifact set (manifest.json + report.md +
   7: bibliography.json + contradictions.json + verification_details.json +
   8: evidence_pool.json). This module translates one to the other.
   9: 
  10: Sovereignty cascade (Codex iter-2 P1-001): non-cleared sources are excluded
  11: from pool.sources entirely. Sentences whose provenance_tokens cite an
  12: excluded source are marked verifier_pass=False + drop_reason="invalid_token"
  13: + evaluator_agrees=False. Sections with no remaining passing sentences are
  14: marked section_status="dropped". If ALL sections drop, raises
  15: SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError so the endpoint can return 422.
  16: """

  54: # I-arch-001d Codex iter-3 P2: import canonical regex if available; else local
  55: # fallback that matches generator2.provenance shape.
  56: try:
  57:     from polaris_graph.generator2.provenance import (  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
  58:         PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE as _CANONICAL_TOKEN_RE,
  59:     )
  60:     _PROV_TOKEN_RE = _CANONICAL_TOKEN_RE
  61: except ImportError:
  62:     # NOTE: align with polaris_graph.generator2.provenance shape:
  63:     # [#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]
  64:     _PROV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:([^:\]]+):(\d+)-(\d+)\]")
  65: 
  66: 
  67: class SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError(RuntimeError):
  68:     """Raised when sovereignty cascade leaves no non-dropped sections.
  69: 
  70:     The endpoint catches this and returns 422 to the client. The
  71:     bundle would otherwise fail `assert_all_pool_sources_legal_cleared`
  72:     or generate an empty pipeline_verdict='success' report — both
  73:     audit-grade incorrect.
  74:     """
  75: 
  76: 
  77: # I-arch-001d Codex iter-4 P2: expanded drop_reason map covering pipeline-A's
  78: # observed reasons including no_integer_overlap / no_content_word_overlap /
  79: # trial_name_mismatch.
  80: _DROP_REASON_MAP: dict[str, DropReason] = {
  81:     "invalid_token": "invalid_token",
  82:     "span_out_of_range": "span_out_of_range",
  83:     "numeric_mismatch": "numeric_mismatch",
  84:     "number_not_in_any_cited_span": "numeric_mismatch",
  85:     "no_integer_overlap_any_cited_span": "numeric_mismatch",
  86:     "overlap_too_low": "overlap_too_low",
  87:     "low_content_overlap": "overlap_too_low",
  88:     "no_content_word_overlap_any_cited_span": "overlap_too_low",
  89:     "no_provenance_token": "no_provenance_token",
  90:     "missing_provenance": "no_provenance_token",
  91:     "entailment_failed": "entailment_failed",
  92:     "trial_name_mismatch": "invalid_token",
  93: }
  94: 
  95: 
  96: def _normalize_drop_reason(raw: str | None) -> DropReason | None:
  97:     if raw is None:
  98:         return None
  99:     base = raw.split(":")[0].strip().lower()
 100:     return _DROP_REASON_MAP.get(base, "invalid_token")
 101: 
 102: 
 103: def _normalize_tier(raw: str) -> tuple[SourceTier, str]:
 104:     """Map pipeline-A tier to SourceTier Literal. T4+/UNKNOWN → T3."""
 105:     upper = (raw or "").upper().strip()
 106:     if upper in ("T1", "T2", "T3"):
 107:         return SourceTier(upper), upper
 108:     return SourceTier.T3, upper or "UNKNOWN"
 109: 
 110: 
 111: def _derive_scope_class(domain: str | None, scope_class: str | None) -> ScopeClassValue:
 112:     """Derive a valid ScopeClassValue Literal from pipeline-A manifest fields."""
 113:     if scope_class:
 114:         s = scope_class.strip().lower()
 115:         for valid in (
 116:             "clinical_efficacy", "clinical_safety", "clinical_diagnosis",
 117:             "clinical_prognosis", "out_of_scope", "uncertain",
 118:         ):
 119:             if s == valid:
 120:                 return valid  # type: ignore[return-value]
 121:     if domain and "clinical" in domain.lower():
 122:         return "clinical_efficacy"
 123:     return "uncertain"
 124: 
 125: 
 126: def _redact_sentence(sent: VerifiedSentence) -> VerifiedSentence:
 127:     """Mark a sentence as failed during sovereignty cascade.
 128: 
 129:     Rebuilds via constructor (runs validators) instead of model_copy
 130:     which bypasses them. Sets evaluator_agrees=False to satisfy the
 131:     invariant that verifier_pass=False forbids evaluator_agrees=True.
 132:     """
 133:     fields = sent.model_dump()
 134:     fields["verifier_pass"] = False
 135:     fields["drop_reason"] = "invalid_token"
 136:     fields["evaluator_agrees"] = False
 137:     return VerifiedSentence(**fields)
 138: 
 139: 
 140: def _evidence_ids_in_tokens(provenance_tokens: list[str]) -> set[str]:
 141:     out: set[str] = set()
 142:     for tok in provenance_tokens:
 143:         m = _PROV_TOKEN_RE.search(tok)
 144:         if m:
 145:             out.add(m.group(1))
 146:     return out
 147: 
 148: 
 149: def _tokens_to_strings(tokens: tuple[EvidenceSpanToken, ...]) -> list[str]:
 150:     return [f"[#ev:{t.evidence_id}:{t.start}-{t.end}]" for t in tokens]
 151: 
 152: 
 153: def _read_optional_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | list[Any] | None:
 154:     if not path.is_file():
 155:         return None
 156:     try:
 157:         return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 158:     except json.JSONDecodeError:
 159:         return None
 160: 
 161: 
 162: def _full_text_for_evidence_id(
 163:     evidence_id: str,
 164:     evidence_pool_raw: dict[str, Any] | list[Any] | None,
 165: ) -> str | None:
 166:     """Lookup evidence body in evidence_pool.json (Codex iter-2 P2 Q4).
 167: 
 168:     Pipeline-A's persisted field is commonly `direct_quote`, not full_text.
 169:     """
 170:     if evidence_pool_raw is None:
 171:         return None
 172:     sources = (
 173:         evidence_pool_raw.get("sources") if isinstance(evidence_pool_raw, dict)
 174:         else evidence_pool_raw
 175:     )
 176:     if not isinstance(sources, list):
 177:         return None
 178:     for src in sources:
 179:         if not isinstance(src, dict):
 180:             continue
 181:         if src.get("evidence_id") == evidence_id or src.get("source_id") == evidence_id:
 182:             return (
 183:                 src.get("full_text")
 184:                 or src.get("direct_quote")
 185:                 or src.get("snippet")
 186:             )
 187:     return None
 188: 
 189: 
 190: def _parse_iso(value: Any) -> datetime | None:
 191:     if not value:
 192:         return None
 193:     if isinstance(value, datetime):
 194:         return value
 195:     try:
 196:         return datetime.fromisoformat(str(value).replace("Z", "+00:00"))
 197:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
 198:         return None
 199: 
 200: 
 201: def build_slice_chain(
 202:     artifact_dir: Path,
 203: ) -> tuple[ScopeDecision, EvidencePool, SliceChainVerifiedReport]:
 204:     """Convert a pipeline-A canonical artifact dir to slice-chain Pydantic models.
 205: 
 206:     Raises:
 207:         FileNotFoundError: artifact_dir missing required canonical files
 208:         SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError: sovereignty cascade leaves no
 209:             non-dropped sections (cannot ship a bundle for this run)
 210:     """
 211:     air = load_audit_ir(artifact_dir)
 212:     manifest_raw = json.loads((artifact_dir / "manifest.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 213:     evidence_pool_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "evidence_pool.json")
 214: 
 215:     # ─── 1. ScopeDecision ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
 216:     decision_id = (
 217:         (manifest_raw.get("scope") or {}).get("decision_id")
 218:         or manifest_raw.get("external_run_id")
 219:         or str(uuid.uuid4())
 220:     )
 221:     scope_class = _derive_scope_class(
 222:         manifest_raw.get("domain"),
 223:         (manifest_raw.get("scope") or {}).get("classification"),
 224:     )
 225:     decision = ScopeDecision(
 226:         decision_id=decision_id,
 227:         status="in_scope",
 228:         scope_class=scope_class,
 229:         ambiguity_axes=[],
 230:         clarifications_needed=[],
 231:         provenance={
 232:             "classifier_layer": "pipeline_a_v30",
 233:             "artifact_dir": str(artifact_dir),
 234:         },
 235:     )
 236: 
 237:     # ─── 2. EvidencePool (sovereignty-filtered) ───────────────────────────
 238:     sources: list[Source] = []
 239:     cleared_evidence_ids: set[str] = set()
 240:     for entry in air.bibliography:
 241:         # Tier-1 is legal-cleared by default; non-T1 requires explicit clearance
 242:         # in evidence_pool.json provenance.legal_cleared = True (Phase 2 hook).
 243:         canonical_tier, raw_tier = _normalize_tier(entry.tier)
 244:         is_legal_cleared = canonical_tier == SourceTier.T1
 245:         if not is_legal_cleared:
 246:             continue  # exclude — sovereignty_guard would reject otherwise
 247:         full_text = _full_text_for_evidence_id(entry.evidence_id, evidence_pool_raw)
 248:         sources.append(Source(
 249:             source_id=entry.evidence_id,
 250:             url=entry.url,
 251:             domain=_url_domain(entry.url),
 252:             tier=canonical_tier,
 253:             title=entry.statement[:200] or "Untitled",
 254:             snippet=entry.statement[:1000] or "n/a",
 255:             full_text_available=full_text is not None,
 256:             full_text=full_text,
 257:             provenance={
 258:                 "legal_cleared": True,
 259:                 "raw_tier": raw_tier,
 260:             },
 261:             retracted=False,
 262:         ))
 263:         cleared_evidence_ids.add(entry.evidence_id)
 264: 
 265:     retrieval_block = manifest_raw.get("retrieval", {}) or {}
 266:     started_utc = _parse_iso(retrieval_block.get("started_at")) or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
 267:     finished_utc = _parse_iso(retrieval_block.get("finished_at")) or started_utc
 268:     pool = EvidencePool(
 269:         pool_id=retrieval_block.get("pool_id") or str(uuid.uuid4()),
 270:         decision_id=decision.decision_id,
 271:         sources=sources,
 272:         adequacy=AdequacyVerdict(
 273:             is_adequate=True,
 274:             adequacy_score=1.0,
 275:             failure_reason=None,
 276:         ),
 277:         queries_executed=retrieval_block.get("queries_executed", []) or [],
 278:         retrieval_started_at_utc=started_utc,
 279:         retrieval_finished_at_utc=finished_utc,
 280:         latency_ms=int(retrieval_block.get("latency_ms") or 0),
 281:         cost_usd=float(air.manifest.cost_usd or 0.0),
 282:     )
 283: 
 284:     # ─── 3. VerifiedReport (sovereignty cascade) ──────────────────────────
 285:     sections: list[Section] = []
 286:     for air_section in air.verified_report.sections:
 287:         kept: list[VerifiedSentence] = []
 288:         for air_sent in air_section.sentences:
 289:             sc_sent = _air_sentence_to_slice_chain(air_sent)
 290:             cited_ids = _evidence_ids_in_tokens(sc_sent.provenance_tokens)
 291:             if not cited_ids or cited_ids.issubset(cleared_evidence_ids):
 292:                 kept.append(sc_sent)
 293:             else:
 294:                 kept.append(_redact_sentence(sc_sent))
 295:         pass_count = sum(1 for s in kept if s.verifier_pass)
 296:         section_status: SectionStatus = (
 297:             "dropped" if pass_count == 0 else "verified"
 298:         )
 299:         sections.append(Section(
 300:             section_id=_slugify(air_section.title) or f"section_{len(sections)}",
 301:             section_title=air_section.title or f"Section {len(sections)}",
 302:             verified_sentences=kept,
 303:             section_verify_pass_rate=pass_count / max(len(kept), 1),
 304:             section_status=section_status,
 305:         ))
 306: 
 307:     non_dropped = [s for s in sections if s.section_status != "dropped"]
 308:     if not non_dropped:
 309:         raise SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError(
 310:             "every section dropped after sovereignty cascade; bundle cannot be assembled"
 311:         )
 312: 
 313:     overall = sum(s.section_verify_pass_rate for s in non_dropped) / len(non_dropped)
 314:     pipeline_verdict: PipelineVerdict = (
 315:         "success" if air.manifest.status == "success" else "abort_no_verified_sections"
 316:     )
 317:     models_block = manifest_raw.get("models") or {}
 318:     report = SliceChainVerifiedReport(
 319:         report_id=air.manifest.run_id,
 320:         pool_id=pool.pool_id,
 321:         decision_id=decision.decision_id,
 322:         sections=sections,
 323:         overall_verify_pass_rate=overall,
 324:         pipeline_verdict=pipeline_verdict,
 325:         generator_model=models_block.get("generator") or "unknown",
 326:         evaluator_model=models_block.get("evaluator") or "strict_verify_v1",
 327:         family_segregation_passed=True,
 328:         verifier_pass_threshold=0.4,
 329:         started_at_utc=started_utc,
 330:         finished_at_utc=finished_utc,
 331:         latency_ms=int(retrieval_block.get("latency_ms") or 0),
 332:         cost_usd=float(air.manifest.cost_usd or 0.0),
 333:     )
 334:     return decision, pool, report
 335: 
 336: 
 337: def _air_sentence_to_slice_chain(air_sent: AuditIRSentence) -> VerifiedSentence:
 338:     """AuditIR ReportSentence → slice-chain VerifiedSentence."""
 339:     failure = air_sent.failure_reasons[0] if air_sent.failure_reasons else None
 340:     return VerifiedSentence(
 341:         section_id=_slugify(air_sent.section) or "section",
 342:         sentence_text=air_sent.text or "n/a",
 343:         provenance_tokens=_tokens_to_strings(air_sent.tokens),
 344:         verifier_pass=bool(air_sent.is_verified),
 345:         drop_reason=_normalize_drop_reason(failure) if not air_sent.is_verified else None,
 346:         evaluator_agrees=bool(air_sent.is_verified),
 347:         is_synthesis_claim=len(air_sent.tokens) == 0 and bool(air_sent.is_verified),
 348:     )
 349: 
 350: 
 351: def _url_domain(url: str) -> str:
 352:     """Extract host for the Source.domain field. Falls back to 'unknown'."""
 353:     from urllib.parse import urlparse
 354:     try:
 355:         return urlparse(str(url)).netloc or "unknown"
 356:     except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — defensive: never raise during bridge
 357:         return "unknown"
 358: 
 359: 
 360: def _slugify(text: str) -> str:
 361:     return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9_]+", "_", (text or "").lower()).strip("_")[:60]

 succeeded in 743ms:
   1: """GET /runs/{run_id}/bundle — F15 audit bundle export.
   2: 
   3: This is the artifact Carney's office receives. Per docs/blockers.md §5,
   4: real bundle redistribution policy is gated on Phase 1/2 legal IP review;
   5: for COPYRIGHTED sources the bundle exports citations + DOI links only,
   6: not verbatim spans.
   7: 
   8: Phase 0 ships the endpoint contract that returns an EvidenceContract
   9: v1.0 JSON. Phase 1 wires it to the actual run-storage backend; here we
  10: serve a synthetic bundle from the golden corpus when a known run_id
  11: matches, otherwise 404.
  12: 
  13: I-arch-001d (2026-05-13) adds GET /runs/{run_id}/bundle.tar.gz which
  14: resolves run_id → artifact_dir via run_store and returns a real
  15: GPG-signed audit bundle via the build_slice_chain bridge.
  16: """
  17: 
  18: from __future__ import annotations
  19: 
  20: import json
  21: from pathlib import Path
  22: 
  23: from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
  24: 
  25: from polaris_v6.api.artifact_to_slice_chain import (

  32: router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["bundle"])

  34: _FIXTURE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "tests" / "v6" / "fixtures" / "evidence_contract_v1"
  35: 
  36: _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX = {
  37:     "golden_clinical_001": "golden_run_clinical.json",
  38:     "golden_housing_002": "golden_run_with_contradiction.json",
  39:     "golden_abort_003": "golden_run_abort_no_verified.json",

  46: 
  47: @router.get("/{run_id}/bundle", response_model=EvidenceContract)
  48: def get_bundle(run_id: str) -> EvidenceContract:
  49:     fixture_name = _GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX.get(run_id)
  50:     if fixture_name is None:
  51:         raise HTTPException(
  52:             status_code=404,
  53:             detail=f"Bundle for run {run_id!r} not found. Available golden runs: {list(_GOLDEN_RUN_INDEX)}",
  54:         )
  55:     raw = json.loads((_FIXTURE_DIR / fixture_name).read_text())
  56:     return EvidenceContract.model_validate(raw)
  57: 
  58: 
  59: @router.get("/{run_id}/bundle.tar.gz")
  60: def get_run_bundle_targz(
  61:     run_id: str,
  62:     sign_fn=Depends(  # I-arch-001d Codex iter-2 P1-004: explicit Depends
  63:         # Lazy import: audit_bundle_route depends on polaris_graph; we don't
  64:         # want bundle.py import to fail if that subtree has runtime issues
  65:         # outside this endpoint.
  66:         lambda: _resolve_sign_fn()
  67:     ),
  68: ):
  69:     """Resolve run_id → artifact_dir → signed audit bundle (tar.gz).
  70: 
  71:     Status codes:
  72:         200: bundleable run; returns application/gzip
  73:         404: run not found, not completed, or artifact_dir missing
  74:         422: run aborted (pipeline_status=abort_*) or sovereignty cascade
  75:              emptied the report
  76:         503: GPG signer not configured (POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID unset)
  77:     """
  78:     info = run_store.get_run(run_id)
  79:     if info is None:
  80:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail={"error": "run not found"})
  81:     if info.lifecycle_status != "completed":
  82:         raise HTTPException(
  83:             status_code=404,
  84:             detail={"error": f"run not completed: lifecycle_status={info.lifecycle_status}"},
  85:         )
  86:     if info.pipeline_status and info.pipeline_status.startswith("abort_"):
  87:         raise HTTPException(
  88:             status_code=422,
  89:             detail={"error": f"run aborted: pipeline_status={info.pipeline_status}", "bundleable": False},
  90:         )
  91:     if not info.artifact_dir:
  92:         raise HTTPException(
  93:             status_code=404, detail={"error": "run has no artifact_dir recorded"}
  94:         )
  95: 
  96:     artifact_dir = Path(info.artifact_dir)
  97:     if not artifact_dir.is_dir():
  98:         raise HTTPException(
  99:             status_code=404,
 100:             detail={"error": f"artifact_dir does not exist on disk: {artifact_dir}"},
 101:         )
 102: 
 103:     try:
 104:         decision, pool, report = build_slice_chain(artifact_dir)
 105:     except SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError as exc:
 106:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail={"error": str(exc)}) from exc
 107:     except FileNotFoundError as exc:
 108:         raise HTTPException(
 109:             status_code=404,
 110:             detail={"error": f"artifact_dir incomplete: {exc}"},
 111:         ) from exc
 112: 
 113:     # Lazy import keeps top-of-file imports clean of polaris_graph dependency.
 114:     from polaris_graph.api.audit_bundle_route import (
 115:         AuditBundleRequest,
 116:         post_audit_bundle,
 117:     )
 118: 
 119:     return post_audit_bundle(
 120:         AuditBundleRequest(decision=decision, pool=pool, report=report),
 121:         sign_fn=sign_fn,
 122:     )
 123: 
 124: 
 125: def _resolve_sign_fn():
 126:     """Lazy import of audit_bundle_route.get_sign_fn for FastAPI Depends.
 127: 
 128:     The audit_bundle_route module imports polaris_graph; deferring keeps
 129:     bundle.py loadable even when the polaris_graph subtree has an init
 130:     issue (it doesn't today, but the lazy pattern is defensive).
 131:     """
 132:     from polaris_graph.api.audit_bundle_route import get_sign_fn
 133:     return get_sign_fn()

 107: 
 108:     # Verification details — sections list with kept[] / dropped[] keys per loader._parse_verified_report.

 111:         kept_sentences = []

 132:         })

 succeeded in 884ms:
   1: """Load a V30 Phase-2 run artifact directory into a canonical AuditIR object.
   2: 
   3: The AuditIR is the single source of truth for the Evidence Inspector and
   4: all derivative renderers. It joins:
   5: - manifest.json (run metadata, frame_coverage_report, corpus tier fractions,
   6:   evaluator gate, v30 warnings, retrieval stats)
   7: - report.md (the rendered markdown report with [N] inline citations)
   8: - bibliography.json (the [N] -> evidence_id -> source mapping)
   9: - contradictions.json (the 14 tier-labeled disagreement clusters with severity)
  10: - verification_details.json (per-section sentences + evidence-span tokens —
  11:   this is the foundation for Evidence Inspector View 1 click-to-inspect)
  12: 
  13: into a single immutable object with lookup methods.
  14: 
  15: Per FINAL_PLAN.md (jointly Claude+Codex agreed):
  16: - The audit graph IR is canonical (NOT the report)
  17: - Evidence Inspector is the primary renderer
  18: - All other outputs are derivative projections with back-links to claim IDs
  19: 
  20: Fail-loud semantics: missing or malformed required schema blocks raise
  21: loudly. There is no silent zero-fill on canonical structures. This is the
  22: audit-grade discipline applied to the IR itself.
  23: """
  24: 
  25: from __future__ import annotations
  26: 
  27: import json
  28: from dataclasses import dataclass
  29: from pathlib import Path
  30: from types import MappingProxyType
  31: from typing import Any, Mapping
  32: 
  33: # Bumped on schema-breaking changes. Renderers should check this and refuse
  34: # to render IR they don't know how to project.
  35: IR_SCHEMA_VERSION = "1.0.0"
  36: 
  37: 
  38: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  39: # Bibliography
  40: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  41: 
  42: 
  43: @dataclass(frozen=True)
  44: class BibliographyEntry:
  45:     """A single source in the bibliography — the [N] -> source mapping."""
  46: 
  47:     num: int
  48:     evidence_id: str
  49:     statement: str
  50:     tier: str
  51:     url: str
  52: 
  53: 
  54: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  55: # Verified report (verification_details.json)
  56: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  57: 
  58: 
  59: @dataclass(frozen=True)
  60: class EvidenceSpanToken:
  61:     """A `[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]` token bound to a sentence."""
  62: 
  63:     evidence_id: str
  64:     start: int
  65:     end: int
  66: 
  67: 
  68: @dataclass(frozen=True)
  69: class ReportSentence:
  70:     """One sentence in the verified report — kept or dropped.
  71: 
  72:     `claim_id` is stable for the same artifact_dir: `<section>:<status>:<idx>`.
  73:     Renderers (Evidence Inspector view 1) overlay these IDs onto the rendered
  74:     `report.md` prose and use them as the click-to-inspect handle.
  75:     """
  76: 
  77:     claim_id: str
  78:     section: str
  79:     text: str
  80:     tokens: tuple[EvidenceSpanToken, ...]
  81:     is_verified: bool
  82:     failure_reasons: tuple[str, ...]
  83: 
  84: 
  85: @dataclass(frozen=True)
  86: class ReportSection:
  87:     """One section in the verified report — title plus all its sentences."""
  88: 
  89:     title: str
  90:     kept_count: int
  91:     dropped_count: int
  92:     total_in: int
  93:     dropped_due_to_failure: int
  94:     sentences: tuple[ReportSentence, ...]
  95: 
  96: 
  97: @dataclass(frozen=True)
  98: class VerifiedReport:
  99:     """The full per-sentence verified report.
 100: 
 101:     `drop_reason_counts` is a read-only mapping from reason -> count.
 102:     """
 103: 
 104:     sections: tuple[ReportSection, ...]
 105:     sentences_verified: int
 106:     sentences_dropped: int
 107:     drop_reason_counts: Mapping[str, int]
 108: 
 109: 
 110: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 111: # Contradictions
 112: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 113: 
 114: 
 115: @dataclass(frozen=True)
 116: class ContradictionClaim:
 117:     """One claim inside a contradiction cluster."""
 118: 
 119:     evidence_id: str
 120:     subject: str
 121:     predicate: str
 122:     arm: str
 123:     dose: str
 124:     value: float
 125:     unit: str
 126:     source_tier: str
 127:     source_url: str
 128:     context_snippet: str
 129:     endpoint_phrase: str
 130: 
 131: 
 132: @dataclass(frozen=True)
 133: class ContradictionCluster:
 134:     """A tier-labeled disagreement cluster — extended with severity + action."""
 135: 
 136:     cluster_id: int
 137:     subject: str
 138:     predicate: str
 139:     severity: str
 140:     absolute_difference: float
 141:     relative_difference: float
 142:     recommended_action: str
 143:     claims: tuple[ContradictionClaim, ...]
 144:

 146: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 147: # Frame coverage
 148: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 149: 
 150: 
 151: @dataclass(frozen=True)
 152: class RetrievalAttempt:
 153:     """One retrieval attempt against an external source — frozen leaf."""
 154: 
 155:     attempt_index: int
 156:     source: str
 157:     url: str
 158:     outcome: str
 159:     http_status: int | None
 160: 
 161: 
 162: @dataclass(frozen=True)
 163: class FrameCoverageEntry:
 164:     """One contract-slot entity in the frame coverage report.
 165: 
 166:     Includes section / slot_id / subsection_title (back-link into report
 167:     structure) and min_fields_for_completion / human_curated_provenance
 168:     (operator-completion workflow inputs).
 169:     """
 170: 
 171:     entity_id: str
 172:     entity_type: str
 173:     section: str
 174:     slot_id: str
 175:     subsection_title: str
 176:     status: str
 177:     doi: str | None
 178:     pmid: str | None
 179:     failure_reason: str | None
 180:     available_artifacts: tuple[str, ...]
 480: 
 481: 
 482: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 483: # Loader internals
 484: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 485: 
 486: 
 487: class AuditIRSchemaError(ValueError):
 488:     """Raised when an artifact directory violates the canonical schema."""
 489: 
 490: 
 491: def _read_json(path: Path) -> Any:
 492:     if not path.exists():
 493:         raise FileNotFoundError(f"Required artifact missing: {path}")
 494:     with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 495:         return json.load(f)
 496: 
 497: 
 498: def _read_text(path: Path) -> str:
 499:     if not path.exists():
 500:         raise FileNotFoundError(f"Required artifact missing: {path}")
 501:     return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
 502: 
 503: 
 504: def _require_keys(d: Mapping[str, Any], keys: tuple[str, ...], where: str) -> None:
 505:     missing = [k for k in keys if k not in d]
 506:     if missing:
 507:         raise AuditIRSchemaError(
 508:             f"{where}: required keys missing: {missing}"
 509:         )
 510: 
 511: 
 512: def _parse_bibliography(raw: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> tuple[BibliographyEntry, ...]:
 513:     if not isinstance(raw, list):
 514:         raise AuditIRSchemaError("bibliography.json: expected a list")
 515:     out = []
 516:     for i, entry in enumerate(raw):
 517:         _require_keys(entry, ("num", "evidence_id"), f"bibliography[{i}]")
 518:         out.append(
 519:             BibliographyEntry(
 520:                 num=int(entry["num"]),
 521:                 evidence_id=str(entry["evidence_id"]),
 522:                 statement=str(entry.get("statement", "")),
 523:                 tier=str(entry.get("tier", "UNKNOWN")),
 524:                 url=str(entry.get("url", "")),
 525:             )
 526:         )
 527:     return tuple(out)
 528: 
 529: 
 530: def _parse_contradiction_claim(
 531:     raw: Mapping[str, Any], where: str
 532: ) -> ContradictionClaim:
 533:     _require_keys(raw, ("evidence_id", "predicate", "value"), where)
 534:     return ContradictionClaim(
 535:         evidence_id=str(raw["evidence_id"]),
 536:         subject=str(raw.get("subject", "")),
 537:         predicate=str(raw["predicate"]),
 538:         arm=str(raw.get("arm", "")),
 539:         dose=str(raw.get("dose", "")),
 540:         value=float(raw["value"]),
 541:         unit=str(raw.get("unit", "")),
 542:         source_tier=str(raw.get("source_tier", "UNKNOWN")),
 543:         source_url=str(raw.get("source_url", "")),
 544:         context_snippet=str(raw.get("context_snippet", "")),
 545:         endpoint_phrase=str(raw.get("endpoint_phrase", "")),
 546:     )
 547: 
 548: 
 549: def _parse_contradictions(
 550:     raw: list[dict[str, Any]],
 551: ) -> tuple[ContradictionCluster, ...]:
 552:     if not isinstance(raw, list):
 553:         raise AuditIRSchemaError("contradictions.json: expected a list")
 554:     clusters = []
 555:     for idx, raw_cluster in enumerate(raw):
 556:         where = f"contradictions[{idx}]"
 557:         _require_keys(raw_cluster, ("predicate", "claims"), where)
 558:         claims_raw = raw_cluster["claims"]
 559:         if not isinstance(claims_raw, list) or len(claims_raw) < 2:
 560:             raise AuditIRSchemaError(
 561:                 f"{where}: 'claims' must be a list of >=2 entries"
 562:             )
 563:         claims = tuple(
 564:             _parse_contradiction_claim(c, f"{where}.claims[{j}]")
 565:             for j, c in enumerate(claims_raw)
 566:         )
 567:         clusters.append(
 568:             ContradictionCluster(
 569:                 cluster_id=idx,
 570:                 subject=str(raw_cluster.get("subject", "")),
 571:                 predicate=str(raw_cluster["predicate"]),
 572:                 severity=str(raw_cluster.get("severity", "unknown")),
 573:                 absolute_difference=float(raw_cluster.get("absolute_difference", 0.0)),
 574:                 relative_difference=float(raw_cluster.get("relative_difference", 0.0)),
 575:                 recommended_action=str(raw_cluster.get("recommended_action", "")),
 576:                 claims=claims,
 577:             )
 578:         )
 579:     return tuple(clusters)
 580: 
 581: 
 582: def _parse_retrieval_attempt(raw: Mapping[str, Any]) -> RetrievalAttempt:
 583:     return RetrievalAttempt(
 584:         attempt_index=int(raw.get("attempt_index", 0)),
 585:         source=str(raw.get("source", "")),
 586:         url=str(raw.get("url", "")),
 587:         outcome=str(raw.get("outcome", "")),
 588:         http_status=(int(raw["http_status"]) if raw.get("http_status") is not None else None),
 589:     )
 590: 
 591: 
 592: def _parse_frame_coverage_entry(
 593:     raw: Mapping[str, Any], where: str
 594: ) -> FrameCoverageEntry:
 595:     _require_keys(raw, ("entity_id", "status"), where)
 596:     return FrameCoverageEntry(
 597:         entity_id=str(raw["entity_id"]),
 598:         entity_type=str(raw.get("entity_type", "")),
 599:         section=str(raw.get("section", "")),
 600:         slot_id=str(raw.get("slot_id", "")),
 601:         subsection_title=str(raw.get("subsection_title", "")),
 602:         status=str(raw["status"]),
 603:         doi=raw.get("doi"),
 604:         pmid=raw.get("pmid"),
 605:         failure_reason=raw.get("failure_reason"),
 606:         available_artifacts=tuple(raw.get("available_artifacts", [])),
 607:         required_fields=tuple(raw.get("required_fields", [])),
 608:         min_fields_for_completion=int(raw.get("min_fields_for_completion", 0)),
 609:         provenance_class=str(raw.get("provenance_class", "")),
 610:         human_completion_eligible=bool(raw.get("human_completion_eligible", False)),
 611:         human_curated_provenance=raw.get("human_curated_provenance"),
 612:         is_pipeline_fault=bool(raw.get("is_pipeline_fault", False)),
 613:         retrieval_attempt_log=tuple(
 614:             _parse_retrieval_attempt(r)
 615:             for r in raw.get("retrieval_attempt_log", [])
 616:         ),
 617:     )
 618: 
 619: 
 620: def _parse_frame_coverage(
 700:     if isinstance(queries_raw, list):
 701:         for q in queries_raw:
 702:             if isinstance(q, str):
 703:                 queries.append(q)
 704:             elif isinstance(q, Mapping) and "query" in q:
 705:                 queries.append(str(q["query"]))
 706:     return RetrievalStats(
 707:         pre_filter=int(raw.get("pre_filter", 0)),
 708:         fetched=int(raw.get("fetched", 0)),
 709:         failed=int(raw.get("failed", 0)),
 710:         by_provider=MappingProxyType(by_provider),
 711:         queries=tuple(queries),
 712:     )
 713: 
 714: 
 715: def _parse_adequacy(raw: Any) -> AdequacyGate | None:
 716:     if not isinstance(raw, Mapping):
 717:         return None
 718:     return AdequacyGate(
 719:         decision=str(raw.get("decision", "")),
 720:         findings_ok=int(raw.get("findings_ok", 0)),
 721:         findings_total=int(raw.get("findings_total", 0)),
 722:         critical_count=int(raw.get("critical_count", 0)),
 723:     )
 724: 
 725: 
 726: def _parse_corpus_approval(raw: Any) -> CorpusApprovalGate | None:
 727:     """Load corpus_approval.json. Returns None if file is absent."""
 728:     if not isinstance(raw, Mapping):
 729:         return None
 730:     return CorpusApprovalGate(
 731:         approved=bool(raw.get("approved", False)),
 732:         decision_at_iso=str(raw.get("decision_at_iso", "")),
 733:         user_note=str(raw.get("user_note", "")),
 734:         approved_count=len(raw.get("approved_source_urls") or []),
 735:         rejected_count=len(raw.get("rejected_source_urls") or []),
 736:     )
 737: 
 738: 
 739: def _parse_completeness_percent(raw: Any) -> float:
 740:     """Compute completeness as a percentage, 0.0–100.0.
 741: 
 742:     V30 manifests use either:
 743:       - {covered_fraction: 0.0–1.0}
 744:       - {total_covered: int, total_applicable: int}
 745:       - older keys: {covered_topics, total_topics} or {covered, total}
 746: 
 747:     Codex M-1 review caught that the old code looked for keys that don't
 748:     exist in the V30 schema, returning 0.0 for a 7/7 manifest.
 749:     """
 750:     if not isinstance(raw, Mapping):
 751:         return 0.0
 752:     if "covered_fraction" in raw:
 753:         try:
 754:             return float(raw["covered_fraction"]) * 100.0
 755:         except (TypeError, ValueError):
 756:             pass
 757:     for cov_key, tot_key in (
 758:         ("total_covered", "total_applicable"),
 759:         ("covered_topics", "total_topics"),
 760:         ("covered", "total"),
 761:     ):
 762:         if cov_key in raw and tot_key in raw:
 763:             try:
 764:                 cov = float(raw[cov_key])
 765:                 tot = float(raw[tot_key])
 766:                 if tot > 0:
 767:                     return (cov / tot) * 100.0
 768:             except (TypeError, ValueError):
 769:                 continue
 770:     return 0.0
 771: 
 772: 
 773: def _parse_manifest(raw: Mapping[str, Any]) -> RunManifest:
 774:     _require_keys(
 775:         raw,
 776:         (
 777:             "run_id",
 778:             "slug",
 779:             "status",
 780:             "question",
 781:             "protocol_sha256",
 782:             "evaluator_gate",
 783:             "completeness",
 784:         ),
 785:         "manifest.json",
 786:     )
 787:     generator = raw.get("generator", {})
 788:     if not isinstance(generator, Mapping):
 789:         generator = {}
 790:     return RunManifest(
 791:         run_id=str(raw["run_id"]),
 792:         slug=str(raw["slug"]),
 793:         status=str(raw["status"]),
 794:         question=str(raw["question"]),
 795:         protocol_sha256=str(raw["protocol_sha256"]),
 796:         cost_usd=float(raw.get("cost_usd", 0.0)),
 797:         budget_cap_usd=float(raw.get("budget_cap_usd", 0.0)),
 798:         word_count=int(generator.get("words", 0)),
 799:         sentences_verified=int(generator.get("sentences_verified", 0)),
 800:         sentences_dropped=int(generator.get("sentences_dropped", 0)),
 801:         contradictions_found=int(raw.get("contradictions_found", 0)),
 802:         completeness_percent=_parse_completeness_percent(raw["completeness"]),
 803:         evaluator_gate=_parse_evaluator_gate(raw["evaluator_gate"]),
 804:         release_allowed=bool(raw.get("release_allowed", False)),
 805:         v30_enabled=bool(raw.get("v30_enabled", False)),
 806:         v30_warnings=tuple(str(w) for w in raw.get("v30_warnings", [])),
 807:         retrieval_stats=_parse_retrieval_stats(raw.get("retrieval")),
 808:     )
 809: 
 810: 
 811: def _parse_verification_token(raw: Mapping[str, Any]) -> EvidenceSpanToken:
 812:     return EvidenceSpanToken(
 813:         evidence_id=str(raw.get("evidence_id", "")),
 814:         start=int(raw.get("start", 0)),
 815:         end=int(raw.get("end", 0)),
 816:     )
 817: 
 818: 
 819: def _parse_verification_sentence(
 820:     raw: Mapping[str, Any], section_title: str, status: str, idx: int
 940:         research_question=str(raw.get("research_question", "")),
 941:         created_at_iso=str(raw.get("created_at_iso", "")),
 942:         created_at_unix=float(raw.get("created_at_unix", 0.0)),
 943:         scope_decision=str(raw.get("scope_decision", "")),
 944:         expected_tier_distribution=expected_tiers,
 945:     )
 946: 
 947: 
 948: def _read_optional_json(path: Path) -> Any:
 949:     """Read a JSON file but return None if it doesn't exist (vs failing loud)."""
 950:     if not path.exists():
 951:         return None
 952:     with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 953:         return json.load(f)
 954: 
 955: 
 956: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 957: # Public API
 958: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 959: 
 960: 
 961: def load_audit_ir(artifact_dir: Path | str) -> AuditIR:
 962:     """Load a V30 Phase-2 run artifact directory into a canonical AuditIR.
 963: 
 964:     Required files in artifact_dir:
 965:         manifest.json
 966:         report.md
 967:         bibliography.json
 968:         contradictions.json
 969:         verification_details.json
 970: 
 971:     Raises
 972:     ------
 973:     NotADirectoryError
 974:         If `artifact_dir` is not an existing directory.
 975:     FileNotFoundError
 976:         If any required file is missing.
 977:     AuditIRSchemaError
 978:         If any required schema block is missing or malformed. The IR fails
 979:         loud at load-time per audit-grade discipline; renderers should not
 980:         have to defensively fill in missing data.
 981:     """
 982:     artifact_dir = Path(artifact_dir)
 983:     if not artifact_dir.is_dir():
 984:         raise NotADirectoryError(f"artifact_dir is not a directory: {artifact_dir}")
 985: 
 986:     manifest_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "manifest.json")
 987:     if not isinstance(manifest_raw, Mapping):
 988:         raise AuditIRSchemaError("manifest.json: top-level must be a JSON object")
 989:     report_md = _read_text(artifact_dir / "report.md")
 990:     bibliography_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "bibliography.json")
 991:     contradictions_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "contradictions.json")
 992:     verification_raw = _read_json(artifact_dir / "verification_details.json")
 993: 
 994:     # Optional provenance files — present in V30 Phase-2 runs, absent in legacy.
 995:     eval_rules_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "evaluator_rule_checks.json")
 996:     qwen_judge_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "qwen_judge_output.json")
 997:     protocol_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "protocol.json")
 998:     corpus_approval_raw = _read_optional_json(artifact_dir / "corpus_approval.json")
 999: 
1000:     manifest = _parse_manifest(manifest_raw)
1001:     bibliography = _parse_bibliography(bibliography_raw)
1002:     contradictions = _parse_contradictions(contradictions_raw)
1003:     frame_coverage = _parse_frame_coverage(
1004:         manifest_raw.get("frame_coverage_report"),
1005:         manifest.v30_warnings,
1006:     )
1007:     tier_mix = _parse_tier_mix(manifest_raw.get("corpus"))
1008:     verified_report = _parse_verified_report(verification_raw)
1009:     model_provenance = _parse_model_provenance(eval_rules_raw, qwen_judge_raw)
1010:     protocol = _parse_protocol(protocol_raw)
1011:     adequacy = _parse_adequacy(manifest_raw.get("adequacy"))
1012:     corpus_approval = _parse_corpus_approval(corpus_approval_raw)
1013: 
1014:     return AuditIR(
1015:         ir_schema_version=IR_SCHEMA_VERSION,
1016:         run_id=manifest.run_id,
1017:         artifact_dir=artifact_dir,
1018:         report_md=report_md,
1019:         manifest=manifest,
1020:         bibliography=bibliography,
1021:         contradictions=contradictions,
1022:         frame_coverage=frame_coverage,
1023:         tier_mix=tier_mix,
1024:         verified_report=verified_report,
1025:         model_provenance=model_provenance,

 succeeded in 910ms:
   1: """VerifiedReport schema for slice 003 (generator + strict-verify).
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_003/architecture_proposal.md` §"verified_report.py".
   4: 
   5: Pure-types module. No I/O, no LLM, no network. Slice 003's
   6: process_generation() consumes an EvidencePool (slice 002 output) and
   7: produces a VerifiedReport, which slice 004 will export as an audit
   8: bundle.
   9: 
  10: Lives in `polaris_graph.generator2` (note the `2`) to keep separation
  11: from heritage `polaris_graph.generator` (multi_section, live_deepseek)
  12: which serves the legacy honest-rebuild pipeline. Coexistence by design
  13: per PLAN.md §4 Heritage Import.
  14: """
  15: 
  16: from __future__ import annotations
  17: 
  18: import uuid
  19: from datetime import datetime, timezone
  20: from typing import Literal
  21: 
  22: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator, model_validator
  23: 
  24: 
  25: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  26: # Type literals
  27: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  28: 
  29: SectionStatus = Literal["verified", "regenerated", "dropped"]
  30: """Per-section outcome from strict_verify:
  31: 
  32: - "verified": original generation passed >=verifier_pass_threshold of
  33:   sentences strict-verified
  34: - "regenerated": original failed; regeneration attempt passed
  35: - "dropped": both attempts failed; section excluded from final report
  36: """
  37: 
  38: PipelineVerdict = Literal["success", "abort_no_verified_sections"]
  39: """Top-level verdict per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 4."""
  40: 
  41: AssertionSurface = Literal[
  42:     "prose",
  43:     "table",
  44:     "summary_bullet",
  45:     "limitation",
  46:     "caption",
  47:     "heading",
  48: ]
  49: """Render-surface kind of a verified assertion (I-f5-009).
  50: 
  51: Per Carney plan §F: every assertion-bearing surface in a verified report
  52: must be gated-and-clickable through the Inspector OR explicitly marked
  53: ungated. Today's generator emits only "prose"; future Issues populate
  54: the other 5 surfaces as the generator learns to label table cells,
  55: summary bullets, etc."""
  56: 
  57: DropReason = Literal[
  58:     "invalid_token",       # token doesn't reference a known source_id
  59:     "span_out_of_range",   # span_start > span_end or > len(full_text)
  60:     "numeric_mismatch",    # decimal in sentence not present in cited span
  61:     "overlap_too_low",     # < min_content_overlap shared content words
  62:     "no_provenance_token", # sentence has no [#ev:...:start-end] tokens
  63:     "entailment_failed",   # I-bug-092: span does not entail sentence (semantic check)
  64: ]
  65: 
  66: 
  67: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  68: # VerifiedSentence — atomic verified unit
  69: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  70: 
  71: class VerifiedSentence(BaseModel):
  72:     """A single sentence emitted by the generator after strict-verify.
  73: 
  74:     Sentences that fail strict_verify are still represented here with
  75:     `verifier_pass=False` and a `drop_reason`, so the audit bundle can
  76:     surface "what got proposed and dropped" alongside "what shipped."
  77:     """
  78: 
  79:     section_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
  80:     sentence_text: str = Field(min_length=1)
  81:     provenance_tokens: list[str] = Field(
  82:         default_factory=list,
  83:         description="Token strings of form '[#ev:<source_id>:<start>-<end>]'",
  84:     )
  85:     verifier_pass: bool
  86:     drop_reason: DropReason | None = None
  87:     evaluator_agrees: bool | None = Field(
  88:         default=None,
  89:         description=(
  90:             "Two-family evaluator agreement per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 1. "
  91:             "True = evaluator confirms generator claim; False = evaluator "
  92:             "disagrees; None = pending (no evaluator pass yet). At rule-based "
  93:             "stage this mirrors verifier_pass; future Issue plugs in the "
  94:             "real two-family LLM judge to populate this independently."
  95:         ),
  96:     )
  97:     assertion_surface: AssertionSurface = Field(
  98:         default="prose",
  99:         description=(
 100:             "Render-surface kind per I-f5-009. Default 'prose' preserves "
 101:             "back-compat with all existing fixtures. Non-prose values are "
 102:             "populated by future generator wiring (table cells, summary "
 103:             "bullets, limitations, captions, headings); the Inspector gates "
 104:             "all six surfaces equally."
 105:         ),
 106:     )
 107:     evaluator_disagreement: "EvaluatorDisagreement | None" = Field(
 108:         default=None,
 109:         description=(
 110:             "Optional detail when evaluator_agrees=False (I-f9-002). "
 111:             "Carries the evaluator's competing reading of the same evidence "
 112:             "so the EvaluatorPane can render generator-vs-evaluator side-by-side."
 113:         ),
 114:     )
 115:     contradiction: "ContradictionSignal | None" = Field(
 116:         default=None,
 117:         description=(
 118:             "Optional contradiction signal (I-f8-001). When present, "
 119:             "≥2 sources cited for this sentence disagree on the claim. "
 120:             "Default None preserves back-compat. Future Issue (F8-002+) "
 121:             "wires real detection into the generator."
 122:         ),
 123:     )
 124:     is_synthesis_claim: bool = Field(
 125:         default=False,
 126:         description=(
 127:             "True iff sentence is a synthesis claim (e.g., a discussion "
 128:             "paragraph summarizing across sources without quoting any one). "
 129:             "Synthesis claims have no provenance_tokens by definition; "
 130:             "non-synthesis kept sentences MUST have ≥1 provenance token. "
 131:             "Today's generator defaults to False; future Issue may wire the "
 132:             "prompt template to label synthesis sentences explicitly."
 133:         ),
 134:     )
 135: 
 136:     @model_validator(mode="after")
 137:     def _drop_reason_consistency(self) -> "VerifiedSentence":
 138:         if self.verifier_pass is False and self.drop_reason is None:
 139:             raise ValueError(
 140:                 "drop_reason is required when verifier_pass=False"
 141:             )
 142:         if self.verifier_pass is True and self.drop_reason is not None:
 143:             raise ValueError(
 144:                 "drop_reason must be None when verifier_pass=True"
 145:             )
 146:         return self
 147: 
 148:     @model_validator(mode="after")
 149:     def _evaluator_agreement_consistency(self) -> "VerifiedSentence":
 150:         if self.verifier_pass is False and self.evaluator_agrees is True:
 151:             raise ValueError(
 152:                 "evaluator_agrees=True is forbidden when verifier_pass=False "
 153:                 "(rule-based dropped the sentence; evaluator cannot pass it "
 154:                 "without contradicting strict-verify)"
 155:             )
 156:         return self
 157: 
 158:     @model_validator(mode="after")
 159:     def _synthesis_claim_consistency(self) -> "VerifiedSentence":
 160:         if self.is_synthesis_claim and self.verifier_pass is False:
 161:             raise ValueError(
 162:                 "is_synthesis_claim=True requires verifier_pass=True "
 163:                 "(synthesis claims either ship or are dropped before reaching "
 164:                 "this record)"
 165:             )
 166:         if self.is_synthesis_claim and len(self.provenance_tokens) > 0:
 167:             raise ValueError(
 168:                 "is_synthesis_claim=True requires provenance_tokens=[] "
 169:                 "(synthesis claims have no specific provenance — that's the "
 170:                 "definition)"
 171:             )
 172:         if (
 173:             not self.is_synthesis_claim
 174:             and self.verifier_pass is True
 175:             and len(self.provenance_tokens) == 0
 176:         ):
 177:             raise ValueError(
 178:                 "kept non-synthesis sentence requires ≥1 provenance token; "
 179:                 "set is_synthesis_claim=True for prose without specific "
 180:                 "provenance"
 181:             )
 182:         return self
 183:

 190:     "canada",

 382: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 383: # Section
 384: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 385: 
 386: class Section(BaseModel):
 387:     """A report section (e.g. 'Population', 'Outcomes').
 388: 
 389:     `verified_sentences` includes both passed AND dropped sentences for
 390:     audit completeness; UI filters by `verifier_pass` when rendering the
 391:     user-facing report. `section_verify_pass_rate` is over all attempted
 392:     sentences (not just kept ones).
 393:     """
 394: 
 395:     section_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
 396:     section_title: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
 397:     verified_sentences: list[VerifiedSentence] = Field(default_factory=list)
 398:     section_verify_pass_rate: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
 399:     section_status: SectionStatus
 400: 
 401:     def kept_sentences(self) -> list[VerifiedSentence]:
 402:         """Filter to sentences that passed strict_verify."""
 403:         return [s for s in self.verified_sentences if s.verifier_pass]
 404: 
 405: 
 406: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 407: # VerifiedReport
 408: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 409: 
 410: class VerifiedReport(BaseModel):
 411:     """The slice 003 output. Bound to a slice-002 pool via pool_id +
 412:     a slice-001 decision via decision_id. Same pool can produce different
 413:     reports across runs (LLM nondeterminism); report_id distinguishes them.
 414:     """
 415: 
 416:     report_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
 417:     pool_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
 418:     decision_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
 419:     sections: list[Section] = Field(default_factory=list)
 420:     overall_verify_pass_rate: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
 421:     pipeline_verdict: PipelineVerdict
 422:     generator_model: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
 423:     evaluator_model: str = Field(
 424:         min_length=1,
 425:         max_length=200,
 426:         description=(
 427:             "Two-family evaluator identifier per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 1. "
 428:             "Rule-based stage value: 'strict_verify_v1'. When a real LLM "
 429:             "judge wires in (separate Issue), this becomes the OpenRouter "
 430:             "model id from a different training family than generator_model."
 431:         ),
 432:     )
 433:     family_segregation_passed: bool = Field(
 434:         default=True,
 435:         description=(
 436:             "True iff generator and evaluator are confirmed to be from "
 437:             "different training lineages (rule-based always True; future "
 438:             "two-family LLM judge invokes "
 439:             "openrouter_client.check_family_segregation)."
 440:         ),
 441:     )
 442:     verifier_pass_threshold: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
 443:     started_at_utc: datetime
 444:     finished_at_utc: datetime
 445:     latency_ms: int = Field(ge=0)
 446:     cost_usd: float = Field(ge=0.0)
 447:     frame_coverage: FrameCoverage | None = Field(
 448:         default=None,
 449:         description=(
 450:             "Optional top-of-report frame coverage summary (I-f7-001). "
 451:             "When present, the Inspector renders a panel above-the-fold "
 452:             "showing 'X of N entities covered' + each gap with reason. "
 453:             "Default None preserves back-compat with older payloads."
 454:         ),
 455:     )
 456: 
 457:     @field_validator("finished_at_utc")
 458:     @classmethod
 459:     def _finished_after_started(cls, v: datetime, info) -> datetime:
 460:         started = info.data.get("started_at_utc")
 461:         if started is not None and v < started:
 462:             raise ValueError(
 463:                 "finished_at_utc must be >= started_at_utc"
 464:             )
 465:         return v
 466: 
 467:     @model_validator(mode="after")
 468:     def _verdict_consistency(self) -> "VerifiedReport":
 469:         """If verdict=success, at least one section must be verified or
 470:         regenerated. If verdict=abort_no_verified_sections, every section
 471:         (if any) must be 'dropped' or sections may be empty."""
 472:         if self.pipeline_verdict == "success":
 473:             kept = [
 474:                 s for s in self.sections if s.section_status != "dropped"
 475:             ]
 476:             if not kept:
 477:                 raise ValueError(
 478:                     "pipeline_verdict='success' requires at least one "
 479:                     "non-dropped section"
 480:                 )
 481:         else:  # abort_no_verified_sections
 482:             for section in self.sections:
 483:                 if section.section_status != "dropped":
 484:                     raise ValueError(
 485:                         "pipeline_verdict='abort_no_verified_sections' "
 486:                         "requires all sections (if any) to be dropped"
 487:                     )
 488:         return self
 489: 
 490:     def kept_sections(self) -> list[Section]:
 491:         """Filter to non-dropped sections."""
 492:         return [s for s in self.sections if s.section_status != "dropped"]
 493:

   1: """EvidencePool schema for slice 002 (clinical retrieval).
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_002/architecture_proposal.md` §"Data contracts".
   4: Defines the canonical Pydantic types produced by the slice 002 clinical
   5: retriever and consumed by future slice 003 generation:
   6: 
   7:     ScopeDecision (slice 001)
   8:         ↓
   9:     process_retrieval()  (slice 002 — orchestrator, later PR)
  10:         ↓
  11:     EvidencePool (this module)
  12:         ↓
  13:     [slice 003 generator — future]
  14: 
  15: Pure-types module: no I/O, no network, no LLM calls. Validation only.
  16: 
  17: Slice 002 lives in `polaris_graph.retrieval2` (note the `2`) to keep
  18: clear separation from the heritage `polaris_graph.retrieval` substrate
  19: (live_retriever, source_registry, etc.) which serves the legacy honest-
  20: rebuild pipeline. The two coexist by design per PLAN.md §4.
  21: """
  22: 
  23: from __future__ import annotations
  24: 
  25: import uuid
  26: from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
  27: from enum import Enum
  28: from typing import Any
  29: 
  30: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, HttpUrl, field_validator, model_validator
  31: 
  32: 
  33: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  34: # Source tier enum
  35: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  36: 
  37: class SourceTier(str, Enum):
  38:     """Three-tier source classification per architecture proposal.
  39: 
  40:     T1 — regulatory + Cochrane systematic reviews. Highest evidentiary weight.
  41:     T2 — peer-reviewed primary research (RCTs, cohort studies, meta-analyses).
  42:     T3 — registries, clinical guidelines, government health agencies.
  43:     """
  44: 
  45:     T1 = "T1"
  46:     T2 = "T2"
  47:     T3 = "T3"
  48: 
  49: 
  50: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  51: # Source
  52: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  53: 
  54: class Source(BaseModel):
  55:     """A single retrieved clinical source.
  56: 
  57:     `full_text` is None at retrieval time and populated lazily by the
  58:     fetcher when downstream stages need the body. `snippet` is always
  59:     populated (first ~500 chars of relevant section) so quick adequacy
  60:     + tier classification can run without a second HTTP round-trip.
  61:     """
  62: 
  63:     source_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
  64:     url: HttpUrl
  65:     domain: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=255)
  66:     tier: SourceTier
  67:     title: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=1000)
  68:     publication_date: date | None = None
  69:     authors: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, max_length=200)
  70:     snippet: str = Field(min_length=0, max_length=2000)
  71:     full_text_available: bool = False
  72:     full_text: str | None = None
  73:     fetched_at_utc: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
  74:     provenance: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
  75:     retracted: bool = Field(
  76:         default=False,
  77:         description=(
  78:             "True iff source has been retracted post-publication "
  79:             "(I-f5-007). Default False — populated by future "
  80:             "CrossRef/Retraction Watch lookup. Today's retrieval "
  81:             "pipeline does not check; field is the surface for that "
  82:             "future wiring."
  83:         ),
  84:     )
  85: 
  86:     @field_validator("domain")
  87:     @classmethod
  88:     def _domain_lowercase(cls, v: str) -> str:
  89:         return v.strip().lower()
  90: 
  91:     @field_validator("authors")
  92:     @classmethod
  93:     def _authors_no_blanks(cls, v: list[str]) -> list[str]:
  94:         return [a.strip() for a in v if a and a.strip()]
  95: 
  96: 
  97: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  98: # Adequacy verdict
  99: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 100: 
 101: class AdequacyVerdict(BaseModel):
 102:     """Output of the corpus adequacy gate.
 103: 
 104:     `is_adequate=True` ⇒ pool may flow to slice 003 generation.
 105:     `is_adequate=False` + `failure_reason` populated ⇒ pipeline aborts
 106:     upstream of generation; UI surfaces the failure_reason verbatim.
 107:     """
 108: 
 109:     is_adequate: bool
 110:     sources_per_tier: dict[SourceTier, int] = Field(default_factory=dict)
 111:     min_required_per_tier: dict[SourceTier, int] = Field(default_factory=dict)
 112:     failure_reason: str | None = None
 113: 
 114:     @model_validator(mode="after")
 115:     def _check_failure_reason_consistency(self) -> "AdequacyVerdict":
 116:         if self.is_adequate is False and not self.failure_reason:
 117:             raise ValueError(
 118:                 "failure_reason is required when is_adequate=False"
 119:             )
 120:         if self.is_adequate is True and self.failure_reason:
 121:             raise ValueError(
 122:                 "failure_reason must be None when is_adequate=True"
 123:             )
 124:         return self
 125:

 127: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 128: # EvidencePool
 129: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 130: 
 131: class EvidencePool(BaseModel):
 132:     """The slice 002 output. Bound to a specific ScopeDecision via decision_id.
 133: 
 134:     `pool_id` is fresh per retrieval run; `decision_id` is the FK back to
 135:     the slice 001 ScopeDecision that triggered retrieval. Same decision +
 136:     same retrieval call may produce different pools across runs (sources
 137:     move, registries update); pool_id distinguishes them.
 138:     """
 139: 
 140:     pool_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
 141:     decision_id: str = Field(min_length=1)
 142:     sources: list[Source] = Field(default_factory=list)

 147:     latency_ms: int = Field(ge=0)
 148:     cost_usd: float = Field(ge=0.0)
 149: 
 150:     @field_validator("retrieval_finished_at_utc")
 151:     @classmethod
 152:     def _finished_after_started(cls, v: datetime, info) -> datetime:
 153:         started = info.data.get("retrieval_started_at_utc")
 154:         if started is not None and v < started:
 155:             raise ValueError(
 156:                 "retrieval_finished_at_utc must be >= retrieval_started_at_utc"
 157:             )
 158:         return v
 159: 
 160:     def sources_by_tier(self, tier: SourceTier) -> list[Source]:
 161:         return [s for s in self.sources if s.tier == tier]

   1: """Scope decision schema + assembly.
   2: 
   3: Per slice 001 architecture proposal §"Data shapes". Defines the canonical
   4: Pydantic types that flow through the front half of the research pipeline:
   5: 
   6:     NormalizedQuestion (intake)
   7:         ↓
   8:     ScopeClass + AmbiguityAxes (classifier + detector — later PRs)
   9:         ↓
  10:     ScopeDecision (assembled here)
  11: 
  12: The schemas exactly match the golden test bundle's expected_scope_decision
  13: shape (polaris-controls/golden/slice_001/test_*.json) so CI can deep-equal.
  14: 
  15: Pure-types module: no I/O, no network, no LLM calls. Validation only.
  16: """
  17: 
  18: from __future__ import annotations
  19: 
  20: import uuid
  21: from datetime import datetime, timezone
  22: from typing import Literal
  23: 
  24: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
  25: 
  26: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  27: # Type literals
  28: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  29: 
  30: PicoAxis = Literal["population", "intervention", "outcome"]
  31: """The three PICO axes used by slice 001's ambiguity detector. Comparator
  32: is intentionally excluded — it's a slice-2+ retrieval concern."""
  33: 
  34: ScopeClassValue = Literal[
  35:     "clinical_efficacy",
  36:     "clinical_safety",
  37:     "clinical_diagnosis",
  38:     "clinical_prognosis",
  39:     "out_of_scope",
  40:     "uncertain",
  41: ]
  42: """Coarse-grained classification of a question's scope. Slice 001 emits
  43: one of these per question; downstream slices may refine or extend."""
  44: 
  45: ScopeStatus = Literal[
  46:     "in_scope",
  47:     "out_of_scope",
  48:     "ambiguous_needs_clarification",
  49:     "refused",
  50: ]
  51: """Final disposition surfaced to the UI. Discriminated-union shape forces
  52: explicit handling of all four cases (rather than booleans + flags). The
  53: golden test suite asserts exactly one of these as expected_scope_decision.status."""
  54: 
  55: 
  56: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  57: # Component schemas
  58: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  59: 
  60: class ScopeClass(BaseModel):
  61:     """Output of the classifier layer."""
  62: 
  63:     value: ScopeClassValue = Field(description="Coarse-grained scope class")
  64:     confidence: float = Field(
  65:         description="Classifier confidence in [0.0, 1.0]; regex hit = 1.0",
  66:         ge=0.0, le=1.0,
  67:     )
  68:     provenance: Literal["regex", "llm_fallback"] = Field(
  69:         description="Which classifier layer produced this verdict"
  70:     )
  71:     matched_pattern: str | None = Field(
  72:         default=None,
  73:         description="For regex hits, the pattern name. Null for LLM fallback.",
  74:     )
  75: 
  76: 
  77: class AmbiguityAxis(BaseModel):
  78:     """One PICO axis's ambiguity assessment."""
  79: 
  80:     axis: PicoAxis = Field(description="Which PICO axis")
  81:     plausible_interpretations: list[str] = Field(
  82:         description="Distinct interpretations the question could plausibly mean"
  83:     )
  84:     needs_clarification: bool = Field(
  85:         description="True iff len(plausible_interpretations) > 1"
  86:     )
  87: 
  88:     @field_validator("plausible_interpretations")
  89:     @classmethod
  90:     def _validate_interpretations_nonempty(cls, v: list[str]) -> list[str]:
  91:         # Empty list is invalid — even a clear axis has 1 interpretation
  92:         if not v:
  93:             raise ValueError("plausible_interpretations must have at least 1 entry")
  94:         # Cap at 5 to keep the modal UI reasonable per architecture proposal
  95:         if len(v) > 5:
  96:             raise ValueError(
  97:                 f"plausible_interpretations capped at 5; got {len(v)}"
  98:             )
  99:         return v
 100:

 102: class AmbiguityAxes(BaseModel):
 103:     """Per-PICO ambiguity. Slice 001 uses three axes (P/I/O); Comparator
 104:     is a slice-2+ retrieval concern."""
 105:

 107:     intervention: AmbiguityAxis
 108:     outcome: AmbiguityAxis
 109:     is_ambiguous: bool = Field(
 110:         description="True iff any axis has needs_clarification=True"
 111:     )
 112: 
 113:     @field_validator("population", "intervention", "outcome")
 114:     @classmethod
 115:     def _validate_axis_matches_field(cls, v: AmbiguityAxis, info) -> AmbiguityAxis:
 116:         if v.axis != info.field_name:
 117:             raise ValueError(
 118:                 f"AmbiguityAxes.{info.field_name} must have axis='{info.field_name}', got '{v.axis}'"
 119:             )
 120:         return v

 819: def _parse_verification_sentence(
 820:     raw: Mapping[str, Any], section_title: str, status: str, idx: int
 821: ) -> ReportSentence:
 822:     return ReportSentence(
 823:         claim_id=f"{section_title}:{status}:{idx}",
 824:         section=section_title,
 825:         text=str(raw.get("sentence", "")),
 826:         tokens=tuple(
 827:             _parse_verification_token(t) for t in raw.get("tokens", [])
 828:         ),
 829:         is_verified=(status == "kept"),
 830:         failure_reasons=tuple(str(r) for r in raw.get("failure_reasons", [])),
 831:     )
 832: 
 833: 
 834: def _parse_verified_report(raw: Mapping[str, Any]) -> VerifiedReport:
 835:     _require_keys(raw, ("sections", "totals"), "verification_details.json")
 836:     if not isinstance(raw["sections"], list):
 837:         raise AuditIRSchemaError("verification_details.json: 'sections' must be a list")
 838:     sections: list[ReportSection] = []
 839:     for sec in raw["sections"]:
 840:         title = str(sec.get("title", ""))
 841:         kept_raw = sec.get("kept", []) or []
 842:         dropped_raw = sec.get("dropped", []) or []
 843:         sentences = list(
 844:             _parse_verification_sentence(s, title, "kept", i)
 845:             for i, s in enumerate(kept_raw)
 846:         )
 847:         sentences.extend(
 848:             _parse_verification_sentence(s, title, "dropped", i)
 849:             for i, s in enumerate(dropped_raw)
 850:         )
 851:         sections.append(
 852:             ReportSection(
 853:                 title=title,
 854:                 kept_count=int(sec.get("total_kept", len(kept_raw))),
 855:                 dropped_count=int(sec.get("total_dropped", len(dropped_raw))),
 856:                 total_in=int(sec.get("total_in", 0)),
 857:                 dropped_due_to_failure=int(sec.get("dropped_due_to_failure", 0)),
 858:                 sentences=tuple(sentences),
 859:             )
 860:         )
 861:     totals = raw.get("totals", {}) or {}
 862:     drop_counts = raw.get("drop_reason_counts", {}) or {}
 863:     return VerifiedReport(
 864:         sections=tuple(sections),
 865:         sentences_verified=int(totals.get("sentences_verified", 0)),

 succeeded in 805ms:
   1: """FastAPI route for slice 004 audit-bundle export.
   2: 
   3: POST /api/audit-bundle — accepts a {decision, pool, report} triple
   4: (typically the slice 001 + 002 + 003 chain output) and streams back
   5: a GPG-signed audit_<bundle_id>.tar.gz file.
   6: 
   7: Mounted alongside the other slice routes in polaris_v6.api.app.
   8: """
   9: 
  10: from __future__ import annotations
  11: 
  12: import tempfile
  13: from pathlib import Path
  14: from typing import Any, Callable
  15: 
  16: from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
  17: from fastapi.responses import FileResponse
  18: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
  19: 
  20: from polaris_graph.audit_bundle.bundle_builder import (
  21:     SignFn,
  22:     build_audit_bundle,
  23: )
  24: from polaris_graph.audit_bundle.bundle_schema import ContentType
  25: from polaris_graph.audit_bundle.manifest_builder import build_manifest_and_files
  26: from polaris_graph.generator2.verified_report import VerifiedReport
  27: from polaris_graph.retrieval2.evidence_pool import EvidencePool
  28: from polaris_graph.scope.scope_decision import ScopeDecision
  29: 
  30: PREVIEW_CONTENT_TYPES: tuple[ContentType, ...] = (
  31:     "scope_decision",
  32:     "evidence_pool",
  33:     "verified_report",
  34:     "source_snapshot",
  35:     "metadata",
  36: )
  37: 
  38: router = APIRouter(tags=["audit-bundle"])
  39: 
  40: 
  41: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  42: # Dependencies
  43: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  44: 
  45: def get_sign_fn() -> SignFn | None:
  46:     """Returns the active GPG signer or None.
  47: 
  48:     None signals the orchestrator's sentinel default which raises and is
  49:     caught by the route -> HTTP 503. Tests override this dep; production
  50:     binds to a real GPGSigner via app.dependency_overrides at startup
  51:     when POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is set.
  52:     """
  53:     return None
  54: 
  55: 
  56: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  57: # Request / response models
  58: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  59: 
  60: class AuditBundleRequest(BaseModel):
  61:     """POST body for /api/audit-bundle.
  62: 
  63:     Carries the full research chain (decision + pool + report). The route
  64:     re-validates the FK chain consistency before building.
  65:     """
  66: 
  67:     decision: ScopeDecision = Field(description="slice 001 ScopeDecision")
  68:     pool: EvidencePool = Field(description="slice 002 EvidencePool")
  69:     report: VerifiedReport = Field(
  70:         description="slice 003 VerifiedReport (verdict=success required)"
  71:     )
  72: 
  73: 
  74: class AuditBundleErrorResponse(BaseModel):
  75:     """Body returned with HTTP 4xx/5xx when bundle cannot be assembled."""
  76: 
  77:     error: bool = True
  78:     code: str  # 'fk_chain_mismatch' | 'verdict_not_success' |
  79:                # 'cited_span_unreachable_after_snapshot' |
  80:                # 'copyrighted_span_in_bundle' |
  81:                # 'gpg_unavailable' | 'sign_failed'
  82:     message: str
  83:     report_id: str | None = None
  84: 
  85: 
  86: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  87: # Routes
  88: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  89: 
  90: @router.post("/audit-bundle")
  91: def post_audit_bundle(
  92:     req: AuditBundleRequest,
  93:     sign_fn: SignFn | None = Depends(get_sign_fn),
  94: ) -> Any:
  95:     """Build + return a GPG-signed audit bundle as application/gzip.
  96: 
  97:     Returns the .tar.gz file as a streaming download.
  98:     """
  99:     if sign_fn is None:
 100:         raise HTTPException(
 101:             status_code=503,
 102:             detail={
 103:                 "error": True,
 104:                 "code": "gpg_unavailable",
 105:                 "message": (
 106:                     "GPG signer not configured. Set POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID in env "
 107:                     "and ensure the corresponding key is in the gpg keyring."
 108:                 ),
 109:                 "report_id": req.report.report_id,
 110:             },
 111:         )
 112: 
 113:     # Build into a temp dir; FileResponse copies it to the wire.
 114:     tmp_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="polaris_audit_"))
 115:     try:
 116:         bundle_path = build_audit_bundle(
 117:             req.decision,
 118:             req.pool,
 119:             req.report,
 120:             output_dir=tmp_dir,
 121:             sign_fn=sign_fn,
 122:         )
 123:     except ValueError as exc:
 124:         # FK chain mismatch, verdict != success, cited-span unreachable, or copyrighted span
 125:         msg = str(exc)
 126:         if "copyrighted span" in msg:
 127:             code = "copyrighted_span_in_bundle"
 128:         elif "cited span unreachable" in msg:
 129:             code = "cited_span_unreachable_after_snapshot"
 130:         elif "FK chain" in msg or "pool_id" in msg or "decision_id" in msg:
 210:             },
 211:         )
 212:     breakdown = {ct: {"count": 0, "bytes": 0} for ct in PREVIEW_CONTENT_TYPES}
 213:     for entry in manifest.files:
 214:         slot = breakdown[entry.content_type]
 215:         slot["count"] += 1
 216:         slot["bytes"] += entry.size_bytes
 217:     return {
 218:         "preview_bundle_id": manifest.bundle_id,
 219:         "generator_model": manifest.generator_model,
 220:         "polaris_version": manifest.polaris_version,
 221:         "file_count": len(manifest.files),
 222:         "total_bytes": manifest.total_bytes(),
 223:         "content_type_breakdown": breakdown,
 224:     }
 225: 
 226: 
 227: @router.get("/audit-bundle/health")
 228: def get_audit_bundle_health(
 229:     sign_fn: SignFn | None = Depends(get_sign_fn),
 230: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 231:     """Liveness probe + slice 004 backend version info.
 232: 
 233:     signing_backend reflects ACTUAL state via the Depends-injected sign_fn:
 234:     - 'sentinel' if no signer is wired (POST will 503 — LAW II fail-loud)
 235:     - 'gpg' if a real GPGSigner is bound via app.dependency_overrides
 236:     """
 237:     return {
 238:         "status": "ok",
 239:         "slice": "slice_004_audit_bundle_export",
 240:         "pipeline_stages": [
 241:             "validate_fk_chain",
 242:             "build_manifest",
 243:             "snapshot_sources",
 244:             "serialize_yaml",
 245:             "gpg_sign",
 246:             "pack_tarball",
 247:         ],
 248:         "signing_backend": "gpg" if sign_fn is not None else "sentinel",
 249:     }

 131: def set_pipeline_meta(
 132:     run_id: str,
 133:     *,

 136:     artifact_dir: str | None = None,

 138:     path: str | None = None,
 139: ) -> None:
 140:     """Set pipeline-A metadata after the actor learns the slug/dir.
 141:

 143:     soon as pipeline-A starts, before the run completes.
 144:     """
 145:     conn = _connect(path)

 148:             "UPDATE runs SET query_slug=COALESCE(?, query_slug), "

 159: def mark_completed(
 160:     run_id: str,
 161:     result: dict[str, Any],
 162:     *,
 163:     pipeline_status: str = "success",
 164:     cost_usd: float | None = None,
 165:     path: str | None = None,
 166: ) -> None:
 167:     """Transition in_progress → completed; set finished_at + result_json + pipeline_status + cost_usd."""
 168:     conn = _connect(path)
 169:     try:
 170:         conn.execute(

 172:             "pipeline_status=?, cost_usd=? WHERE run_id=?",

 180: def mark_aborted(
 181:     run_id: str,
 182:     *,
 183:     pipeline_status: str,
 184:     abort_reason: str,
 185:     cost_usd: float | None = None,
 186:     path: str | None = None,
 187: ) -> None:
 188:     """Pipeline ran to completion but hit a gate; lifecycle_status='completed', pipeline_status='abort_*'.
 189: 
 190:     Per CLAUDE.md §9.3, abort_* are pipeline verdicts (not errors). The run
 191:     completed operationally; the pipeline-A logic chose to halt at a gate.
 192:     """
 193:     conn = _connect(path)
 194:     try:
 195:         conn.execute(
 196:             "UPDATE runs SET lifecycle_status='completed', finished_at=?, "
 197:             "pipeline_status=?, error_json=?, cost_usd=? WHERE run_id=?",
 198:             (
 199:                 _now_iso(),
 200:                 pipeline_status,
 201:                 json.dumps({"abort_reason": abort_reason}, sort_keys=True),
 202:                 cost_usd,
 203:                 run_id,
 204:             ),
 205:         )
 206:         conn.commit()
 207:     finally:
 208:         conn.close()
 209:

 211: def mark_failed(run_id: str, error: str, *, path: str | None = None) -> None:
 212:     """Operational failure (exception, missing manifest, unknown status, etc).
 213: 
 214:     Distinct from mark_aborted: failures are unplanned (exception or invariant
 215:     violation); aborts are planned (pipeline_status='abort_*' written by
 216:     pipeline-A at a gate).
 217:     """
 218:     conn = _connect(path)
 219:     try:
 220:         conn.execute(
 221:             "UPDATE runs SET lifecycle_status='failed', finished_at=?, "
 222:             "pipeline_status='error_unexpected', error_json=? WHERE run_id=?",
 223:             (_now_iso(), json.dumps({"error": error}, sort_keys=True), run_id),
 224:         )
 225:         conn.commit()
 226:     finally:
 227:         conn.close()
